response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project; the rest of his 15+ year career is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies. The role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise alongside industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 secure-development practices, and protocols such as MQTT, OPC UA, and Modbus — none of which appear in his resume.
strengths: Extensive experience designing and operating high-throughput, event-driven distributed systems at scale (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second)
Broad polyglot background including systems-adjacent languages (DLang, C#, Java) and strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Demonstrated mentoring ability and knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and community involvement
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015; listed skills omit C++ entirely
No industrial automation, IoT, or relevant protocol experience (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0) present anywhere in the resume
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle implementation, IEC 62443 compliance, or any industrial cybersecurity practice
